title: Puerto Banus Yacht Charter Wedding: Costs, Licence & Tips for 2026
html:
For a yacht charter wedding in Puerto Banus, you can expect to spend from €20,000 to €80,000 for a full-day crewed vessel departing from the Real Club Náutico de Marbella. BoatHire24 connects you with verified operators who specialise in ceremonies at sea, so you can compare boat hire Puerto Banus options without the stress.

How much does it cost to rent a boat for a wedding in Puerto Banus?

A full-day wedding charter on a 50ft motor yacht starts around €15,000 and can exceed €80,000 on a superyacht. Prices depend on guest count, catering, and length of hire. The figure often includes a captain, crew, and basic wedding package. You can boat hire Puerto Banus for your chosen dates.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

Most wedding charters depart from Puerto Banus itself, but you may also launch from nearby Marbella’s Real Club Náutico, Cabopino Marina west of the port, or the sportier Sotogrande. For a coastal ceremony, head east towards Calahonda or anchor off the island of Isla de las Palomas near Estepona. Each spot offers a different backdrop, from sandy coves to dramatic cliff lines.

Which boat to rent

  1. Motor Yacht (Azimut 60), Best for intimate groups of 12 guests with indoor dining; from €20,000 for an eight-hour charter.
  2. Catamaran (Lagoon 52), Ideal for 20 guests who want stable deck space and water toys; from €25,000 full day.
  3. Classic Schooner (Sunseeker 80), Suits up to 30 guests for a romantic sail; from €35,000.
  4. Superyacht (Mefasa 90), Hosts 40 guests with a dedicated bridal suite; from €50,000. For full specs and availability, you can consult the Mefasa 90 listing.
  5. Flybridge (Fairline 65), Great for 14 guests who want panoramic views; from €22,000.

What are the costs for a yacht charter wedding in Puerto Banus?

  • Shared sunset tour: €80 per person, no ceremony.
  • Small motorboat half-day (up to 8 guests): from €1,500.
  • Catamaran full-day (up to 20 guests): from €6,000.
  • Crewed yacht full-day wedding (up to 40 guests): from €20,000.

Do you need a licence to rent a wedding boat in Puerto Banus?

For a fully crewed charter you do not need a licence; the operator provides the skipper. If you wish to drive the boat yourself, a Spanish Permiso de Navegación or equivalent ICC is required. The operator will confirm exact requirements at time of booking.

Book your wedding charter for May or June to enjoy the Levante wind cooling the deck; it also keeps the infamous afternoon swell at bay.

At a glance

  • Price band: €1,500 to €80,000
  • Peak months: May through September
  • Trip length: half-day (4 hours) to full day (8+)
  • Capacity: 8 to 40 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Can we hold the ceremony on the deck?

Yes, most wedding charters allow a civil ceremony on deck, but you must arrange the legal paperwork with a local judge beforehand.

What happens if the weather is bad on my wedding day?

Operators typically reschedule or refund if the coastguard issues a warning; check the cancellation policy when you compare boat hire Puerto Banus options.

Can we anchor for a swim during the reception?

Yes, many itineraries include a stop at a calm bay such as Cabopino, where guests can swim and enjoy water toys.

How far in advance should we book a wedding charter?

For a peak-summer Saturday wedding, book at least six months ahead to secure the exact vessel you want.

Planning a Puerto Banus wedding charter in 2026 means you have access to a marketplace where every boat is verified against local standards. Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
